Case Study 02

Asthma
Self-Management

Helping patients manage their asthma from home — in collaboration with a pulmonologist at MedStar Georgetown University Hospital.

Asthma

My Role
Lead Product Designer
Collaboration
MedStar Georgetown University Hospital
Platform
iOS Mobile Application
Goal
Reduce reliance on memory for asthma management
The Goal

Empowering patients,
enhancing care

Empower asthma patients to effectively manage their condition from home — while enhancing the accuracy of medical assessments during check-ups with their physician.


The challenge: asthma management is chronic, invisible, and memory-intensive. Most patients aren't failing because they don't care — they're failing because the tools ask too much of them.

Research

From interviews to
common truths

Competitive analysis and user interviews with asthma patients and doctors affiliated with Georgetown University Hospital revealed consistent, systemic challenges across two distinct user groups.

User Journey Map — Asthma Patient
Awareness
Diagnosis
Daily Mgmt
Episode
Recovery
Actions
Notices recurring shortness of breath & wheezing
Visits doctor, undergoes spirometry & allergy tests
Takes medication, tracks symptoms & monitors triggers
Uses rescue inhaler, calls emergency contact
Follows up with doctor, adjusts treatment plan
Thoughts
"Why do I keep coughing at night? Is this serious?"
"This is overwhelming. How do I remember all of this?"
"I forgot my inhaler again. Is my air quality safe today?"
"I can't breathe. I need help fast."
"I need a better system — this can't keep happening."
Emotion
😟 Anxious
😰 Overwhelmed
😓 Frustrated
😱 Fearful
😤 Determined
Pain Points
No awareness of environmental triggers
Too much info to retain from a single appointment
Forgetting meds; no reliable reminder system
Can't reach emergency contacts quickly enough
No record of episode to share with doctor
Opportunity
AQI + weather monitoring in-app
Audio intake & medication label scanning
Smart reminders & daily check-in flow
One-tap emergency contact & SOS feature
Auto-generated report shared with physician
Self-Management Challenges
  • Remembering to take medication consistently
  • Mastering proper inhaler technique
  • Managing appointments and follow-ups
  • Renewing prescriptions before running out
  • Monitoring symptoms consistently over time
Subgroup Accommodations
  • Varying literacy levels across patient demographics
  • Children requiring age-appropriate interactions
  • Users with dementia or memory impairment
  • Patients with ADHD and attention challenges
  • Physical barriers to proper inhaler usage
How Might We

Help asthma patients have a reliable management system to reduce reliance on memory?

Key Features

Designed to
reduce manual effort

In light of the research, patients sought effective asthma monitoring — but also a lifestyle where managing their condition doesn't excessively consume their attention, time, or energy. Three feature categories were prioritized.

Automation
Medication reminders triggered automatically and an automated medicine label reader — eliminating manual data entry for new prescriptions entirely.
Audio Feature
Enables verbal data entry for diverse user preferences, varying literacy levels, and users with physical limitations — making the app genuinely inclusive.
Smartwatch Integration
Passive monitoring of respiratory rate, blood oxygen, and heart rate — delivering data to both patient and physician without any active input required.
In Detail

Four flows that
do the heavy lifting

01
Symptom Tracker

Patients receive a daily check-in notification — friendly and non-intrusive. If symptoms are reported, they're directed to a dedicated page to record them and receive guidance on managing their condition.

02
Adding New Medication

Patients can upload medications by capturing an image of the label, entering details manually, or using voice input — three pathways to accommodate every user type, from tech-savvy to accessibility-dependent.

03
Medication Record

A single location to review all active medications and monitor supply status. Patients can also schedule an auto-email to their doctor for refill approval — removing the friction of manual outreach.

04
Emergency Contact

Urgent contacts are accessible via a single tap on the app's emergency icon — bypassing long contact lists on the user's phone. Designed for high-stress, low-dexterity moments when speed matters most.

Outcome

Peace of mind,
by design

1
App for Everything
Users no longer needed multiple tools to manage their condition — Airy consolidated all asthma needs into a single, cohesive product.
↓
Cognitive Load
Automation, audio input, and passive smartwatch monitoring reduced the mental overhead of daily condition management significantly.
✓
Clinician Alignment
Automated report sharing with doctors improved the accuracy of medical assessments during check-ups — bridging the gap between home and clinic.
What Users Said

Praised for its exclusive focus on comprehensive asthma management — users appreciated that it didn't try to be a general health app.

Emergency contact features and automatic information filling were highlighted as standout differentiators from existing tools.

Minimalistic design was described as motivating for tracking — the inhaler tutorial and timer features were particularly valued for managing severe episodes.

The audio feature for vocal form-filling was intuitive. Users suggested saving notes in audio format for greater accuracy — a feature addition for v2.

"Airy provides users with peace of mind by consolidating all their asthma-related needs into one app. They no longer need to use multiple tools to complete their tasks."

— Rania Tirari, Lead Product Designer
